audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar Arad, bronze serpent, journey stations, well song, and Transjordan battle text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Arad conflict and herem, Edom detour, serpent crisis and bronze-serpent healing sign, travel stations and ancient poems, well song, Sihon and Og battles, and conquest of Amorite territory. Strong anti-colonial, anti-violence, anti-idolatry, and medical-safety safeguards required.

verses.restored: The Canaanite king of Arad, dwelling in Negev, heard that Israel came by way of Atharim; he fought against Israel and took some of them captive.
Israel vowed a vow to YHWH and said, If giving you give this people into my hand, then I will devote their cities to destruction.
YHWH listened to voice of Israel and gave the Canaanite, and they devoted them and their cities; name of place was called Hormah/Devotion.
They set out from Mount Hor by way of Sea of Reeds to go around land of Edom, and soul/life of people became short/impatient on way.
The people spoke against Divine and against Moses: Why have you brought us up from Egypt to die in wilderness? For there is no bread and no water, and our soul/life loathes this miserable bread.
YHWH sent among people fiery serpents, and they bit people, and many people from Israel died.
The people came to Moses and said, We have sinned/misaligned, for we spoke against YHWH and against you. Pray to YHWH that he remove serpent from us. Moses prayed for people.
YHWH said to Moses, Make for yourself a fiery serpent and put it upon a standard; it shall be that everyone bitten, when he sees it, shall live.
Moses made a serpent of bronze and put it upon standard; and it was, if serpent bit a man and he looked toward bronze serpent, he lived.
Children of Israel set out and camped at Oboth.
They set out from Oboth and camped at Iye-abarim, in wilderness facing Moab toward sunrise.
From there they set out and camped in wadi Zered.
From there they set out and camped beyond Arnon, which is in wilderness going out from border of Amorites; for Arnon is border of Moab, between Moab and Amorites.
Therefore it is said in Book of Wars of YHWH: Waheb in Suphah, and wadis of Arnon,
and slope of wadis that turns toward dwelling of Ar and leans upon border of Moab.
From there to Beer/Well; that is well where YHWH said to Moses, Gather people, and I will give them water.
Then Israel sang this song: Rise up, well! Answer to it!
Well that leaders dug, that nobles of people excavated, with scepter, with their staffs. And from wilderness to Mattanah,
and from Mattanah to Nahaliel, and from Nahaliel to Bamoth,
and from Bamoth to valley that is in field of Moab, at top of Pisgah, overlooking face of wasteland.
Israel sent messengers to Sihon king of Amorites, saying,
Let me pass through your land. We will not turn into field or vineyard; we will not drink water of well; by king's road we will go until we pass through your border.
Sihon did not allow Israel to pass through his border; Sihon gathered all his people and went out to meet Israel in wilderness, and came to Jahaz and fought against Israel.
Israel struck him by edge of sword and possessed his land from Arnon to Jabbok, as far as children of Ammon, for border of children of Ammon was strong.
Israel took all these cities, and Israel dwelt in all cities of Amorites, in Heshbon and all its daughters.
For Heshbon was city of Sihon king of Amorites, and he had fought against former king of Moab and taken all his land from his hand as far as Arnon.
Therefore the proverb-makers say: Come to Heshbon; let city of Sihon be built and established.
For fire went out from Heshbon, flame from city of Sihon; it consumed Ar of Moab, lords of high places of Arnon.
Woe to you, Moab! You are lost, people of Chemosh! He gave his sons as fugitives and his daughters into captivity to Sihon king of Amorites.
We shot them; Heshbon perished as far as Dibon, and we laid waste as far as Nophah, which reaches to Medeba.
Israel dwelt in land of Amorites.
Moses sent to scout Jazer; they captured its daughters and dispossessed Amorites who were there.
They turned and went up by way of Bashan, and Og king of Bashan went out to meet them, he and all his people, for battle at Edrei.
YHWH said to Moses, Do not fear him, for into your hand I have given him and all his people and his land; you shall do to him as you did to Sihon king of Amorites, who dwelt at Heshbon.
They struck him and his sons and all his people until no survivor remained to him, and they possessed his land.

translation_notes: The Arad vow and destruction language is herem warfare material and must not be used to justify modern annihilation, ethnic cleansing, or sacred violence.
Israel goes around Edom rather than violating Edom's refusal from chapter 20.

The bronze serpent is a narrative healing sign in response to a serpent crisis; it is not permission for snake handling, magical medicine, or rejection of healthcare. Later tradition records its destruction when it became an object of worship.
The well song preserves communal gratitude and may reflect shared labor by leaders and people.
The chapter includes ancient war poetry celebrating Sihon's conquest of Moab before Israel's conquest of Sihon; these layered voices require literary and historical audit.
Sihon and Og narratives preserve territorial warfare and totalizing victory rhetoric. They must not be mapped onto modern peoples or borders.
Requests for passage do not erase the sovereignty or humanity of peoples already inhabiting the land.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 21 to sanctify genocide, territorial conquest, forced displacement, snake handling, magical healing, or refusal of medical care. Preserve the ancient conflict narrative while clearly rejecting modern violence and centering water, healing, route boundaries, and the humanity of every people named.
